name: Low RAM (system)
summary: Low RAM. $0100-$010F holds the secondary jump table (legacy 6-byte JMPs); $0110+ is unused by Ladybug today.

name: Direct page
summary: Direct-page hot variables. DP register is fixed at $02 in Ladybug. FRAMES (u16 frame counter) at $0202.

name: Low RAM (scratch)
summary: Free RAM for general use; below the stack ceiling.

name: Stack top
summary: Top of hardware stack. S initialised here; stack grows downward.

name: Framebuffer
summary: 320×192×16 framebuffer (30 720 bytes = 3.75 pages). MMU PAR1-4 maps virtual $2000-$9FFF to physical $30-$33. GIME's vertical-offset register $FF9D/$FF9E points at phys $30.

name: FB slack
summary: 2 K of free space at the tail of FB page 3 (FB ends at $97FF). Free for scratch / sprite save-restore buffer.

name: Game-state RAM
summary: PAR5 region. Reserved for Phase 4+ entity tables, score, etc. Not yet populated.

name: Cart window
summary: Cart ROM virtual mapping. On XRoar 1.10 always reads from cart ROM, regardless of TY mode — the self-copy is empirically a no-op on XRoar. On real hardware the post-self-copy switch makes this RAM-backed at phys $3E-$3F.

name: Primary jump table
summary: RAM-backed when Init0 bit 3 = 1 (always, in Ladybug). Long-branch trampolines for the interrupt vectors. Writable.

name: PIA1
summary: Peripheral Interface Adapter 1 — keyboard matrix, cassette, VDG mode bits.

name: PIA2
summary: Peripheral Interface Adapter 2 — joystick comparator + DAC, fire buttons, cartridge FIRQ.

name: FDC / reserved I/O
summary: Floppy-disk controller window ($FF40-$FF5F when a disk cart is present) and reserved I/O ($FF60-$FF8F). Unused by Ladybug — no disk controller in the cart configuration.

name: GIME control
summary: GIME registers: Init0 ($FF90), Init1 ($FF91), IRQ enables ($FF92-93), timer ($FF94-95), video mode ($FF98), video res ($FF99), border ($FF9A), vertical scroll ($FF9C), vertical offset ($FF9D-9E), horizontal offset ($FF9F).

name: MMU PARs
summary: 8 active PARs (PAR0-7 at $FFA0-A7) plus 8 alternate (PAR8-F at $FFA8-AF). Each maps an 8 K virtual page to a 6-bit physical page (0-63 on a 512 K machine).

name: Palette
summary: 16 palette registers (PAL0-PAL15). 6-bit RGB on RGB monitor, 6-bit composite phase on TV. Cleared by GIME reset.

name: SAM legacy
summary: Legacy SAM register addresses. Most-used: $FFD8/$FFD9 = clock rate (0.89/1.78 MHz), $FFDE/$FFDF = ROM-disable/all-RAM (TY) bit.

name: Reserved
summary: Dedicated I/O page; not used by Ladybug today.

name: Hardware vectors
summary: Hardware reset/interrupt vectors. $FFF2-FFFF are mirrored to the primary jump table at $FEEE-$FEFF when Init0 bit 3 is set.
